Kiev/Kyiv adoption in GDELT news — Russian media vs everyone else
30.7%
Non-Russian media
14.3M mentions
1.7%
Russian media
2.3M mentions
18x adoption gap — Russian media almost exclusively uses "Kiev"
Top Holdouts
Media domains still predominantly using "Kiev" over "Kyiv" (2024–2026, min 50 mentions)

In 2018, Ukraine's Ministry of Foreign Affairs launched #KyivNotKiev — a campaign to change how the world spells Ukrainian place names. We built a data pipeline to measure whether it actually worked.

This dashboard tracks 69 Ukrainian toponym pairs (from city names to dishes to historical terms) across 7 independent data sources. Every data point comes from real-world usage — news articles, social media posts, encyclopedia pageviews, book frequency, and web search trends.

Some findings: social media communities adopted Ukrainian spellings 5–9x faster than traditional media. "Disaster brands" like Chernobyl are nearly immovable. And food terms like Borshch are fighting a separate battle, championed by chef Yevhen Klopotenko's UNESCO campaign.

Source Code Research Paper
